channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Johannesburg placed on financial watch amid concerns over delayed statements and service delivery risks
item.description: Johannesburg has been placed on a negative financial watch by Global Credit Rating (GCR) after the city failed to submit audited financial statements on time, raising serious concerns about its financ...

item.title: Club Rose Bay renovation shifts focus from veterans to younger patrons
item.description: Residents in Sydney’s eastern suburbs are expressing frustration over the recent revamp of the Rose Bay RSL, managed by hospitality entrepreneur Justin Hemmes’ Merivale group. Traditionally a venue fo...

item.title: Bunnings and Kmart owner removes delivery fees amid fuel crisis to support customers
item.description: A major Australian retail group that owns well-known chains such as Bunnings and Kmart has announced it will scrap delivery fees as part of a broader response to ongoing pressures linked to a fuel cri...

item.title: PM Modi greets Vice-President Radhakrishnan on the occasion of Puthandu
item.description: On April 14, 2026, Prime Minister Narendra Modi visited Vice-President C.P. Radhakrishnan at his official residence in New Delhi to extend greetings on the occasion of Tamil New Year, known as Puthand...

item.title: Infinity Hills Project Enters Construction Phase After Full Compensation in Zanzibar
item.description: Demolition work has officially started at Kikwajuni in Zanzibar for the Infinity Hills project, marking a major step forward after all compensation payments to former residents were completed. The dev...

item.title: Inquest hears earlier treatment may have saved woman who died of sepsis in Ontario hospital
item.description: An inquest into the 2021 death of 24-year-old Heather Winterstein has heard expert testimony suggesting her life may have been saved if she had received earlier treatment at a St. Catharines, Ontario ...

item.title: United Airlines CEO reportedly discussed potential merger with American Airlines with U.S. officials
item.description: United Airlines CEO Scott Kirby has reportedly raised the idea of a potential combination between United Airlines and American Airlines with multiple U.S. government officials, according to sources fa...

item.title: Marketing Firm Played Key Role in Geese's Rapid Rise
item.description: The Brooklyn-based indie rock band Geese gained rapid fame in late 2025 with their album 'Getting Killed,' selling out tours and appearing on high-profile platforms like Saturday Night Live and Coache...

item.title: Nyanza Cooperative Union Announces 25bn/- Investment to Strengthen Cotton Sector
item.description: The Nyanza Cooperative Union (NCU-1984) has declared plans to invest more than 25 billion Tanzanian shillings during the current financial year, starting this April, in a bid to enhance cooperative gr...

item.title: Government Implements Limited Daily Loadshedding to Manage Fuel Shortages and Control Tariffs
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The government has formally announced that daily electricity loadshedding will be implemented for about 2.25 hours during peak hours, from 5:00pm to 1:00am, as a measure to manage fuel shor...

item.title: B.C. court hears final arguments in challenge over MAID access in faith-based facilities
item.description: Closing arguments have begun in a British Columbia Supreme Court case that could affect whether faith-based health-care facilities in the province may continue to prohibit medical assistance in dying,...

item.title: Senator Ndume Say Nigeria Fit End Insecurity in Six Months If Government Act Fast
item.description: Senator Ali Ndume, wey dey represent Borno South Senatorial District, talk say the ongoing insecurity for Nigeria fit end within six months if the Federal Government take the issue serious. He yarn th...

item.title: Trader behind $380m Russian oil profits expands into Guyana energy market
item.description: A German oil trader who reportedly made around $380 million trading Russian crude following Moscow’s invasion of Ukraine is now turning his attention to South America’s emerging energy hub, Guyana. Ch...

item.title: Philippine Navy recovers suspected cyanide from Chinese boats near Ayungin Shoal
item.description: Crew members of the BRP Sierra Madre, a Philippine Navy ship stationed at Ayungin Shoal, shared their experiences intercepting Chinese fishing boats suspected of carrying cyanide. According to two sai...

item.title: Over 1,000 Hollywood professionals oppose Paramount–Warner Bros. Discovery merger over industry concerns
item.description: More than 1,000 prominent figures from Hollywood, including leading actors, directors, and writers, have jointly expressed strong opposition to the proposed $111 billion merger between Paramount and W...

item.title: PHILTA and Indian Tennis Standouts Set to Compete in 2026 Palawan Open
item.description: The 2026 Palawan Open is shaping up to be one of the most anticipated tennis tournaments in the Philippines this year, as top players from the Philippine Lawn Tennis Association (PHILTA) will be joine...

item.title: Protest in Islamabad’s Noorpur Shahan Injures Two Policemen During CDA Drive
item.description: In Islamabad’s Noorpur Shahan area, tensions escalated as the Capital Development Authority (CDA) launched an anti-encroachment operation. The operation, intended to clear illegal structures, faced st...

item.title: Research Links Early Smell Loss to Alzheimer's Disease
item.description: A recent study conducted by DZNE and Ludwig-Maximilians-Universität München suggests that loss of smell may serve as an early indicator of Alzheimer's disease. The research indicates that Alzheimer’s ...

item.title: Former Duterte aide waives bank secrecy during House impeachment hearing
item.description: Ramil Madriaga, who identified himself as a former aide and alleged bagman of Vice President Sara Duterte, signed a bank secrecy waiver during a hearing of the House of Representatives' justice commit...

item.title: PCB suspends Blessing Muzarabani for two PSL seasons after contract breach
item.description: The Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB) has announced a two-season ban on Zimbabwe’s fast bowler Blessing Muzarabani from participating in the Pakistan Super League (PSL). The decision, made after a detailed...
